satire_attempt: Engineer B has 'shed the bondage of grasping at every promo cycle' — which is what we call it when someone has already been promoted and no longer worries about it. Wait until she gets passed over and watch the substrate stop being 'settled'.
verdict_reason: The satire's setup ('Engineer B is post-promotion, that's why she's settled') is a misreading the analog explicitly heads off: 'The promotion eventually arrives because the engineering is good; the promotion does not move her substrate, because her substrate is no longer downstream of the promotion.' The order is inverted in the cynic's reading. The verse-and-analog claim is that the substrate's settled-ness PRECEDES rather than FOLLOWS the promotion outcome. Both directions of cycle outcome (passed-over and promoted) leave the substrate unmoved — that is the testable claim, not 'Engineer B got promoted and is now relaxed'. The counter-example also forecloses the affective-flatness reading. Pass.
